questions.question: Define the Subset Sum Problem and explain why it is considered NP-Complete.
keyPoints: Problem definition: Given set S and target W, find subset that sums to W
NP-Complete means in NP class and NP-hard
No polynomial-time exact algorithm known (unless P=NP)
Verification of solution is polynomial-time

questions.question: Explain the Dynamic Programming approach for solving Subset Sum. What is its time and space complexity?
keyPoints: Create dp[n+1][W+1] table
dp[i][j] = true if subset of first i elements sums to j
Base cases: dp[0][0]=true, dp[0][j]=false for j>0
Transition: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] OR dp[i-1][j-arr[i-1]]
Time: O(n×W), Space: O(n×W) or O(W) optimized

questions.question: What is a pseudo-polynomial time algorithm? Why is the DP solution for Subset Sum called pseudo-polynomial?
keyPoints: Pseudo-polynomial: polynomial in numeric value, exponential in input bits
DP complexity O(n×W) depends on target value W
W can be exponentially large relative to input size (log W bits)
Not truly polynomial in input representation

questions.question: Explain how backtracking can be used to solve Subset Sum. Discuss its time complexity and pruning strategies.
keyPoints: Recursive: include or exclude each element
Start from array end, two choices at each step
Worst case explores 2^n combinations = O(2^n)
Pruning: stop when sum exceeds target
Pruning: if remaining elements cannot reach target, skip branch
Sorting descending helps effective pruning

questions.question: What is the Partition Problem? How is it related to Subset Sum?
keyPoints: Partition Problem: can set be divided into two equal-sum subsets
Target = total_sum/2
Special case of Subset Sum
Used in load balancing and resource allocation
NP-complete problem

questions.question: Explain the Meet-in-the-Middle approach for Subset Sum.
keyPoints: Divide set into two halves
Compute all subset sums for each half (2^(n/2) each)
Store sums in sorted lists or hash tables
Search for pair of sums (one from each half) that add to target
Time: O(2^(n/2)), Space: O(2^(n/2))

questions.question: What is FPTAS? How does it provide approximation for Subset Sum?
keyPoints: Fully Polynomial Time Approximation Scheme
Provides solution within (1±ε) of optimal
Time polynomial in n and 1/ε
For Subset Sum: scales down values, runs DP, scales up
Useful when exact solution is computationally infeasible

questions.question: Solve the Subset Sum problem for set {3, 34, 4, 12, 5, 2} with target W = 9 using Dynamic Programming. Show the DP table and explain your solution.
keyPoints: Create dp table with dimensions 7×10
Fill base cases
Apply recurrence for each element
Trace path to find subset {4,5} or {3,4,2}
Final answer: YES, subset exists
Time complexity explanation

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Dynamic Programming, Backtracking, and Meet-in-the-Middle approaches for solving Subset Sum. Discuss their time and space complexities, advantages, and limitations.
keyPoints: DP: O(n×W) time, pseudo-polynomial, always finds solution
Backtracking: O(2^n) worst case, exponential, good with pruning
MITM: O(2^(n/2)), better for large n, small W
DP limited by W value, Backtracking by n
MITM space tradeoff vs time
Discuss when each is suitable

questions.question: Explain the concept of NP-Completeness. Prove that Subset Sum is NP-Complete by showing it is in NP and NP-Hard.
keyPoints: P: problems solvable in poly time
NP: problems verifiable in poly time
NP-Complete: in NP and NP-Hard
Subset Sum in NP: given subset, verification O(n) to check sum
Subset Sum NP-Hard: can reduce from known NPC problem (like 3-SAT or Partition)
Karp's 21 NP-complete problems includes Subset Sum
If poly-time solution exists, all NP problems solvable

questions.question: Write and explain the pseudo-code for the DP-based Subset Sum algorithm. Optimize it to use O(W) space and explain the optimization.
keyPoints: Full 2D DP pseudo-code with table initialization
Nested loops for i from 1 to n
Inner loop for j from 0 to W
Recurrence implementation
Space optimization: use 1D array
Iterate j backwards in inner loop to avoid overwriting needed values
Explain why backwards iteration works
